rulings.Case Title: GAIL M. MCMAHON VS. PHILIP A. RICHARDSON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Amend The Second Amended Complaint;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 23, 2011, line 9. PLAINTIFF GAIL MCMAHON'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Amend The Second Amended Complaint. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED ON THE CONDITION THAT PLAINTIFF MAKE HERSELF AVAILABLE FOR AN ADDITIONAL 3 HOURS OF DEPOSITION BY DEFENDANTS CITY MEWS II, RICHARDSON AND JOHNSON AS TO THE NEW ALLEGATIONS IN THE THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT, AND THAT PLAINTIFF MAKE HERSELF AVAILABLE FOR SIX HOURS OF DEPOSITION BY THE THREE DOE DEFENDANTS NAMED IN THE THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT, AS TO THE ALLEGATIONS ASSERTED AS TO THEM. THESE DEPOSITIONS MUST TAKE PLACE BY NO LATER THAN AUGUST 1, 2011. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: DEEANNA M. BROSSOIT VS. DREXEL BRADSHAW et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order That The Truth Of Any Matters Specified In The Request For Admission Be Deemed Admitted; For Order Compelling Plaintiff To Respond To Interrogatories And Document Production Demand For Order Finding Waiver Of Rights; And To Pay Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 23, 2011, line 1. DEFENDANTS DREXEL BRADSHAW AND BRADSHAW & ASSOCIATES, P.C.'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order That The Truth Of Any Matters Specified In The Request For Admission Be Deemed Admitted; For Order Compelling Plaintiff To Respond To Interrogatories And Document Production, Demand For Order Finding Waiver Of Rights; And To Pay Monetary Sanctions. GRANTED, NO OPPOSITION FILED. PLAINTIFF TO PAY $2,500.00 IN SANCTIONS, TO BE PAID WITHIN 15 DAYS OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: ARNULFO BONILLA et al VS. GILBERT M. LEE et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 23, 2011, line 1. DEFENDANT GILBERT LEE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILED TO MAINTAIN ITS SUMMARY JUDGMENT BURDEN. ISSUES RESOLVED IN THE UNDERLYING UNLAWFUL DETAINER ACTION BEFORE JUDGE STEWART ARE UNRELATED TO THE REMAINING CAUSES OF ACTION IN THIS CASE. MOREOVER, THE COURT REJECTS DEFENDANT'S ARGUMENTS REGARDING THE DETERMINATION OF WHETHER A DUTY EXISTED BECAUSE THERE IS NOT SUPPORTING EVIDENCE REGARDING THAT ISSUE. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: RANDY NORTH et al VS. ST. FRANCIS BAY-ONE HUNDRED ONE CRESCENT WAY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 23, 2011, line 3. PLAINTIFFS RANDY NORTH AND SUE YU'S MOTION TO TAX COSTS. PLAINTIFFS' MOTION TO TAX COSTS IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. FIRST, THE MOTION WAS TIMELY FILED AND ACCORDING TO THE PROPER PROCEDURE, SEE CRC 3.1700(A)(1)). SECOND, THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO THE $13.00 PRINTING FEE AND COPYING FEE (CCP 1033.5(B)(3); the $1050.00 LEGAL RESEARCH FEES (CCP 1033.5(A)(10) AND (B)(2)); AND THE $1200 REAL ESTATE RESEARCH FEE (CCP 1033.5(B)(2)). THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO THE FILING FEE FOR THE DEMURRER (CCP 1032(A)(1)) AND THE $250.00 MEDIATION FEE. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: MARCIA GIUSTI et al VS. BKCM ENTERPRISES INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORAION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Defts' Motion For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 23, 2011, line 4. DEFENDANTS JOHN SIMONSE, MAGNATE FUND #1 LLC, MAGNATE FUND #2 LLC, MAGNATE FUND #3 LLC, 27TH STREET ASSOCIATES LLC, 20 PARKRIDGE LLC, SOUTH VAN NESS ASSOCIATES LLC, LHJS INVESTMENTS,LLC AND JWS CAPITAL MANAGEMENT, INC.'S Notice Of Motion For Protective Order. DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER IS GRANTED IN PART. DEFENDANTS HAVE ESTABLISHED GOOD CAUSE FOR THE PROTECTIVE ORDER, AS TO THEIR FINANCIAL DOCUMENTS. THE PROTECTIVE ORDER SHALL BE LIMITED TO THOSE PROPERTIES AND LOANS AT ISSUE IN THIS COMPLAINT AND SHALL PREVENT DISSEMINATION OF THE INFORMATION TO NON-PARTIES. THE PROTECTIVE ORDER SHALL INCLUDE LANGUAGE THAT TESTIMONY GIVEN AT A DEPOSITION MAY BE DESIGNATED AS CONFIDENTIAL BY MAKING A STATEMENT TO THAT EFFECT AT THE TIME OF THE DEPOSITION AND ONLY AS TO QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS SPECIFICALLY DIRECTED TO ISSUES INVOLVING DEFENDANTS' FINANCIAL RECORDS. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: MALFRED C. SPENCER VS. GOLDEN GATE BRIDGE HIGHWAY & TRANSPORTATION et al

rulings.Case Title: MARA MURILLO VS. ABM INDUSTRIES INCORPORATED, et al*****CASE TRANSFERRED TO FRESNO COUNTY*****
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Transfer Of Venue [Ccp 397(C)]
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 23, 2011, line 4. DEFENDANT ABM INDUSTRIES INCORPORATED AND ABM SERVICES, INC (ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS ABM)'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Transfer Of Venue [Ccp 397(C)]. DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR TRANSFER OF VENUE PER CCP 397(C) IS GRANTED. UNDER JUNEAU, A PARTY OPPOSING A TRANSFER OF VENUE MOTION MUST PROVIDE A DECLARATION SHOWING THE 5 ELEMENTS TO SHOW WHY A CHANGE OF VENUE WOULD BE IMPROPER. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO DO SO. MOREOVER, EVEN IF, AS PLAINTIFF SUGGESTS, DEFENDANTS WERE REQUIRED TO MAKE A HEIGHTENED SHOWING BY PROVIDING A DECLARATION SUPPORTING GROUNDS FOR THE MOTION, DEFENDANT HERE HAS DONE SO. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: LEWIS BRISBOIS BISGAARD & SMITH LLP, A LIMITED VS. DANIEL L MITCHELL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 23, 2011, line 5. CROSS DEFENDANT LEWIS BRISBOIS BISGAARD & SMITH LLP'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. CROSS-DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO STRIKE PUNITIVE DAMAGES CLAIM IS DENIED. FRAUD CLAIS IS SUFFICIENTLY PLED. MOTION TO STRIKE THE DAMAGES CLAIM IS GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MUST COMPLY WITH REQUIREMENTS OF CCP 403.030 AND 403.060. =(302/LMG)
